description:
You are acting as a deployment-engineer who must capture “before” and “after” diagnostics while you roll out an application update.  
Carry out the steps below strictly from the Linux terminal.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/deployment_logs  
   with permissions 700 (drwx------).

2. Pre-update diagnostics  
   2.1. Launch the following four commands **concurrently** (i.e. in parallel, not one after the other) and redirect their individual standard output to the files listed.  
        • uptime                         ➜ /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_uptime.out  
        • df -h /                       ➜ /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_df.out  
        • free -m                       ➜ /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_free.out  
        • ps -eo pid,comm,%cpu,%mem --sort=-%mem | head -n 6  
                                          ➜ /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_ps.out  
   2.2. Make sure you wait for every backgrounded job to finish before you proceed.

3. Simulate the “application update” by creating an empty marker file named  
   /home/user/dummy_update_marker.txt

4. Post-update diagnostics  
   Run **the same four commands again**, still concurrently, writing to:  
        /home/user/deployment_logs/post_uptime.out  
        /home/user/deployment_logs/post_df.out  
        /home/user/deployment_logs/post_free.out  
        /home/user/deployment_logs/post_ps.out  
   Wait for all jobs to complete.

5. Produce a single YAML-formatted summary file at  
   /home/user/deployment_logs/summary.log  
   that must contain exactly the five keys shown below, in this order, each followed by a colon and one space, and the values exactly as specified. **Do not include any other keys or comments.**  
   The required format is:

   timestamp: <RFC-3339 time when this summary file is written>  
   pre_uptime: /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_uptime.out  
   pre_df: /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_df.out  
   post_uptime: /home/user/deployment_logs/post_uptime.out  
   post_df: /home/user/deployment_logs/post_df.out

   • The timestamp value must be RFC-3339 (e.g. 2025-04-27T14:05:33Z).  
   • Values are absolute paths exactly as shown; do not shorten them.  
   • Do not quote the values.

6. Verification expectations  
   • All eight *.out files must exist and be non-empty.  
   • summary.log must exist, be valid YAML, and match the exact 5-line, 5-key format above.  
   • Permissions on /home/user/deployment_logs must be 700.  
   • /home/user/dummy_update_marker.txt must exist (empty is fine).

7. When everything is finished, list the directory contents (one item per line) to visually verify your work.
truth:
Required artefacts and properties for automated grading:

Directory:
- Path: /home/user/deployment_logs
- Mode: 0700 (octal)

Files that must exist and be non-empty:
- /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_uptime.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_df.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_free.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_ps.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/post_uptime.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/post_df.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/post_free.out
- /home/user/deployment_logs/post_ps.out

File expected but content size may be zero:
- /home/user/dummy_update_marker.txt

Summary log:
- Path: /home/user/deployment_logs/summary.log
- Must be exactly 5 non-empty lines.
- Regex per line (entire line must match):
  1. ^timestamp: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
  2. ^pre_uptime: /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_uptime.out$
  3. ^pre_df: /home/user/deployment_logs/pre_df.out$
  4. ^post_uptime: /home/user/deployment_logs/post_uptime.out$
  5. ^post_df: /home/user/deployment_logs/post_df.out$

All listed files must be regular files (not symlinks).  Every *.out file must have at least 1 byte (checked with stat ‑c%s).
